note: NEGATIVE for the Pareto goal. Combined tiling (016) + ADSampling rerank (034) on the rotated binary funnel. Recall identical (lossless), latency marginally better, but batch QPS DROPS 5-8% (735->687 @C=2000, 579->532 @C=4000). Reason (023 lesson): once tiling makes the scan cheap, rerank dominates, and plain l2_sq rerank is a tight autovectorized loop - ADSampling's batched early-exit branching defeats that vectorization, and the pruned-dims saving doesn't repay it. ADS rerank only helped UNTILED (034) where the scan still dominated. Conclusion: rerank is now random-gather BANDWIDTH-bound, not compute-bound -> the right lever is fewer gather BYTES (bf16), not fewer flops (037).
